text: You should follow the policy first.
You could offer him a mentor.
You should not ignore a grievance.
Murielle, I need your judgment. We have an analyst on probation and his manager sent a complaint. What should we do?
We should not rush. First, we should follow the policy and check the guidelines.
Good point. And if he needs help?
You could offer him a mentor. He might just need more support during the probation period.
And do we have to document all of this?
Yes, we must document everything and stay in full compliance. Every manager is accountable.
And if the problem continues after that?
Then we might have to give a formal warning. But first, we should give him a fair chance.
We must document everything for compliance.

Hi Murielle, it's Liam. I need your professional judgment on something. We have a new analyst on probation, and his manager just sent me a complaint. In my opinion, we shouldn't rush to a decision. I think we should follow the policy first and check the guidelines carefully. You could offer him a mentor for the rest of the probation period, because he might just need more support. We must document everything, of course, and stay in full compliance. If the problem continues, we might have to give a formal warning, but I really think we should give him a fair chance first. What is your recommendation? Please call me back when you can. Thanks.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from London, with a quick update on the new performance policy. All managers must complete the annual appraisal before the end of the quarter. They should prepare examples in advance and they shouldn't use vague comments. Remember, every manager is accountable for a fair process. If two team members disagree during the review, you could ask a senior colleague to mediate. Managers may use their own discretion on small issues, but for anything serious they must speak to HR first. Please remind your team that compliance is not optional. Thank you, and have a great day.
We should follow the policy first.

Every manager must complete the appraisal.
You should not ignore a grievance.
For anything serious, you must speak to HR first.
